compatibility_report:
# Compatibility Report
- Name of the game with compatibility issues: ARK Survival Evolved
- Steam AppID of the game: 346110
 
## System Information
- GPU: RTX 2060
- Driver/LLVM version: Nvidia 470.57.02
- Kernel version: 5.13.4
- Link to full system information report as [Gist](https://gist.github.com/): https://gist.github.com/Curve/29e780e6f6fa5fff5b33e955fa03456e
- Proton version: Experimental

## I confirm:
- [x] that I haven't found an existing compatibility report for this game (that report the same error, there is one with a different issue #3218).
- [x] that I have checked whether there are updates for my system available.

<!-- Please add `PROTON_LOG=1 %command%` to the game's launch options and
attach the generated $HOME/steam-$APPID.log to this issue report as a file.
(Proton logs compress well if needed.)-->
[steam-346110.log](https://github.com/ValveSoftware/Proton/files/6885206/steam-346110.log)


## Symptoms <!-- What's the problem? -->
The game crashes with the following message:
```
Fatal error!



VERSION: 0.0 
UnknownModule!EntryPoint() (0x000000007b01236e) + 0 bytes [UnknownFile:0]
UnknownModule!__delayLoadHelper2() (0x000000014385bcb5) + 0 bytes [UnknownFile:0]
UnknownModule!__tailMerge_ApexFramework_x64_dll() (0x00000001437778d6) + 0 bytes [UnknownFile:0]
UnknownModule!InitGamePhys() (0x0000000142cd9fbe) + 0 bytes [UnknownFile:0]
UnknownModule!FEngineLoop::PreInit() (0x0000000140111311) + 0 bytes [UnknownFile:0]
UnknownModule!GuardedMain() (0x000000014010e3c0) + 0 bytes [UnknownFile:0]
```

The issue seems to only occur since the latest ARK-Update from the 24th, before it ran fine

## Reproduction
Start the Game, the issue occurs before any menus / windows are visible.

<!--
1. You can find the Steam AppID in the URL of the shop page of the game.
   e.g. for `The Witcher 3: Wild Hunt` the AppID is `292030`.
2. You can find your driver and Linux version, as well as your graphics
   processor's name in the system information report of Steam.
3. You can retrieve a full system information report by clicking
   `Help` > `System Information` in the Steam client on your machine.
4. Please copy it to your clipboard by pressing `Ctrl+A` and then `Ctrl+C`.
   Then paste it in a [Gist](https://gist.github.com/) and post the link in
   this issue.
5. Please search for open issues and pull requests by the name of the game and
   find out whether they are relevant and should be referenced above.
-->
symptom_summary:
用户在将《帝国时代4》的语言设置为西班牙语后，游戏无法启动，出现黑屏，游戏鼠标可见，但随后关闭。 

Tags: Game Crash, Black Screen, Language Issue

compatibility_report:
# Compatibility Report
- Name of the game with compatibility issues: Mushihimesama
- Steam AppID of the game: 377860

## System Information
Steam Deck LCD
SteamOS 3.7.9
- Proton version: 9.0

## I confirm:
- [x] that I haven't found an existing compatibility report for this game.
- [x] that I have checked whether there are updates for my system available.

<!-- Please add `PROTON_LOG=1 %command%` to the game's launch options and
attach the generated $HOME/steam-$APPID.log to this issue report as a file.
(Proton logs compress well if needed.)-->

## Symptoms <!-- What's the problem? -->

On Proton 9.0, after launching the game at least once, subsequent launches of the game on the Steam Deck require pressing a button for the game to actually start (I think it's the start button you have to press) or else it sits at a black screen.

On Proton 7.0 I noticed I didn't have this issue, the game boots up without pressing the start button on the Steam Deck and doesn't hang on a blackscreen until the button is pressed.

## Reproduction

Start Mushihimesama with Proton 9.0
Play the game for a bit, then quit (i don't remember if this step is required)
Start the game up again.
Notice black screen.
Press the start button on the Steam Deck.
Notice the game finally boots up.

Play the game in Proton 7.0
Notice you don't have the above issue



<!--
1. You can find the Steam AppID in the URL of the shop page of the game.
   e.g. for `The Witcher 3: Wild Hunt` the AppID is `292030`.
2. You can find your driver and Linux version, as well as your graphics
   processor's name in the system information report of Steam.
3. You can retrieve a full system information report by clicking
   `Help` > `System Information` in the Steam client on your machine.
4. Please copy it to your clipboard by pressing `Ctrl+A` and then `Ctrl+C`.
   Then paste it in a [Gist](https://gist.github.com/) and post the link in
   this issue.
5. Also, please copy the contents of `Help` > `Steam Runtime Diagnostics` to
   the gist.
6. Please search for open issues and pull requests by the name of the game and
   find out whether they are relevant and should be referenced above.
-->
symptom_summary:
用户在使用Proton 9.0启动《Mushihimesama》时，发现游戏在首次启动后，后续启动需要按下按钮才能开始，否则会停留在黑屏状态。而在Proton 7.0中，游戏可以正常启动，无需按任何按钮。

Tags: Black Screen, Button Press Required, Launch Issue

# Compatibility Report
- Name of the game with compatibility issues: Rise of the Tomb Raider
- Steam AppID of the game: 391220

## System Information
- GPU: GTX 1060 & RTX 2070
- Driver/LLVM version: nvidia 460
- Kernel version: 5.11.6
- Link to full system information report as [Gist](https://gist.github.com/): [link](https://gist.github.com/karli-sjoberg/b5d781b601b085b02eff1f014fbf27ee)
- Proton version: 5.13-6 & Proton Experimental

## I confirm:
- [ x ] that I haven't found an existing compatibility report for this game.
- [ x ] that I have checked whether there are updates for my system available.

[steam-391220.log](https://github.com/ValveSoftware/Proton/files/6199021/steam-391220.log)

## Symptoms
Really strange graphical behavior with a halo of sorts emanating from the heads of enemies and friendly NPC's. They say a picture is worth a thousand words, so here's a 1 200 000 word report :smile: 
https://thecool.tube/videos/watch/7ec5b0cf-cb26-434d-91ef-424182a94b6f


## Reproduction
Start the game with DX12 enabled and play until you meet first NPC or enemy. Would've worked around it by just using DX11 instead but the game is unplayable due to ho-o-o-orrible stuttering while processing shader data
symptom_summary:
用户在《古墓丽影：崛起》中遇到奇怪的图形问题，敌人和友方NPC的头部出现光环现象，同时在使用DX11时游戏因处理着色器数据而出现严重的卡顿，导致游戏无法正常进行。

Tags: Graphical Glitches, Stuttering, Halo Effect

compatibility_report: # Compatibility Report - Name of the game with compatibility issues: The Crew Motorfest - Steam AppID of the game: 2698940 ## System Information - GPU: Intel Arc A770m - Video driver version: Mesa 25.2.4 - Kernel version: 6.16.4-116.bazzite.fc42.x86_64 - Link to full system information report as [Gist](https://gist.github.com/): Steam System Information https://gist.github.com/pboard/26aef27c095308fdfec420010a9c0952 Steam Runtime Diagnostics https://gist.github.com/pboard/e444fb80241c6d9908e75db9d9dd3429 - Proton version: Latest Proton Experimental (10.x branch) ## I confirm: - [x] that I haven't found an existing compatibility report for this game. - [x] that I have checked whether there are updates for my system available. PROTON_LOG=1 output (cut output at line number in 20,000 range when it started looping) https://gist.github.com/pboard/e61ce4036697162160beb2f419540258 ## Symptoms When I launch the game, which is now Steam OS compatible in the latest version according to the announcements, I get a Wine C++ Error, loader_thunks.c vkCreatePipelineLayout (picture of crash message attached) ## Reproduction Launch the latest The Crew Motorfest build, which is now listed as having Steam OS support, and it crashes after it gets past loading the Ubisoft Launcher, which starts fine, and then attempts to launch the game.
symptom_summary:
用户在启动《The Crew Motorfest》时遇到Wine C++错误，导致游戏崩溃。错误信息显示在加载Ubisoft启动器后，尝试启动游戏时发生问题。

**Tag Suggestions:** Crash, Wine Error, Launch Failure

compatibility_report:
# Compatibility Report
- Name of the game with compatibility issues: Battlefield 1
- Steam AppID of the game: 1238840

## System Information
- GPU: 1660 Super
- Driver/LLVM version: NVIDIA 470.57.02
- Kernel version: 5.10.53-1-MANJARO
- Link to full system information report as [Gist](https://gist.github.com/): https://gist.github.com/MarioDZN/0448951d76c7cf4466748df004122c32
- Proton version: 6.4-5 / Experimental / 6.14-GE-2 ( Unofficial )

## I confirm:
- [ X] that I haven't found an existing compatibility report for this game.
- [ X] that I have checked whether there are updates for my system available.

<!-- Please add `PROTON_LOG=1 %command%` to the game's launch options and
attach the generated $HOME/steam-$APPID.log to this issue report as a file.
(Proton logs compress well if needed.)-->
[steam-1238840.log](https://github.com/ValveSoftware/Proton/files/6941174/steam-1238840.log)


## Symptoms <!-- What's the problem? -->

The game seems to launch fine, however the mouse is locked to the center from the very start the cursor appears in the game window. I can move the mouse slightly, however it seems to get sent back to the center of the screen every frame. This makes it very difficult to navigate the menus as the only solution is to use the keyboard for navigation. 

The mouse works once in game, however is once again locked to the center once in the pause menu or death screen. I'm able to exit the pause screen and I can aim / play fine, however as mentioned, it's difficult not being able to use the menus.
symptom_summary:
用户在《战地1》中遇到的问题是鼠标光标在游戏窗口中被锁定在中心位置，导致无法正常导航菜单，必须使用键盘进行操作。在游戏中鼠标可以正常使用，但在暂停菜单或死亡屏幕时又被锁定在中心，影响了用户体验。

Tag suggestions: Mouse Lock, Menu Navigation Issue, Gameplay Disruption

compatibility_report:
# Compatibility Report
- Name of the game with compatibility issues: Judgment
- Steam AppID of the game: 2058180

## System Information
- GPU: RTX 3060 TI
- Driver/LLVM version: NVIDIA 515.65.01
- Kernel version: 5.19.8-200.fc36.x86_64
- Link to full system information report as [Gist](https://gist.github.com/): https://gist.github.com/PaulcPaul/ca28c65c0491d7efbe45b670577ede7c
- Proton version: Proton Experimental, 7.0-4 and 6.3-8

## I confirm:
- [x] that I haven't found an existing compatibility report for this game.
- [x] that I have checked whether there are updates for my system available.

<!-- Please add `PROTON_LOG=1 %command%` to the game's launch options and
attach the generated $HOME/steam-$APPID.log to this issue report as a file.
(Proton logs compress well if needed.)-->


## Symptoms <!-- What's the problem? -->
Game hangs in the first "Tailing" mission as soon as the game is supposed to show you how to hide from the person you're supposed to be tailing.

I've tried this on Proton Experimental, 7.0-4 and 6.3-8, all giving the same results.

[steam-2058180-6.3-8.log](https://github.com/ValveSoftware/Proton/files/9570615/steam-2058180-6.3-8.log)
[steam-2058180-7.0-4.log](https://github.com/ValveSoftware/Proton/files/9570617/steam-2058180-7.0-4.log)
[steam-2058180-experimental.log](https://github.com/ValveSoftware/Proton/files/9570619/steam-2058180-experimental.log)

## Reproduction
Simply start the game and play through it normally until you reach the first "Tailing" mission (which is right at the start of the game), wait enough time and the game will hang as it seemingly tries to show you a small tutorial on how to hide from your "victim". The moment I'm talking about can be seen in the following video and timestamp: https://youtu.be/GoMF004uALc?t=823

<!--
1. You can find the Steam AppID in the URL of the shop page of the game.
